Piramal Swasthya is focusing on bridging the gaps of Public Healthcare by supplementing and complementing Government of India’s vision to meet Universal Health Care for all. Piramal Swasthya is one of the largest not-for-profit organizations in India - in the primary public health care space with a focus on Maternal health, Child and Adolescent Health, Non-communicable diseases. Piramal Swasthya has over a decade-long experience in operating several healthcare innovations at scale, which are addressing the primary health care needs of most underserved and marginalized populations across India. Piramal Swasthya is operational in 20 states in India through 35 innovative public health care delivery programs and has served more than 10.7 Crore beneficiaries thus far, with its services designed to reach the last village and the last household. Piramal Swasthya employs 2260 employees including 250 medical doctors who work around the core value of Seva Bhav (beneficiary-centricity) through an outcome oriented approach. Over the last twelve years, the focus has been on, Building platforms for sustainable impact at scale, Innovations to scale as a journey, Influencing policies, governance, and accountability of the public health care system as a knowledge and implementation partner of the NITI Aayog. Innovation in the form of intervention design, modelling, piloting and scaling-up those pilots has been an approach at Piramal Swasthya. Piramal Swasthya is committed to make primary healthcare not only affordable, accessible and available to all, but also to ensure that it is complete, continuous and comprehensive. Powered By
Required field cannot be empty ! Only Alphabets in upper case allowed! Field must contain %1d digits. Only Alphabets and whitespace allowed! Only Alphabets and special characters allowed! Only Alphabets and special characters, digits allowed! %1$s should be greater than or equal to %2$d Only Alphabets and Numerics allowed! ABHA number should be 14-digit long 4 letters followed by 7 digits (e.g., ABCD1234567)! IFSC App Settings Incentive Dashboard Facilitator No Alphabets or spaces allowed! Only Digits allowed! %1$s should be greater than or equal to %2$.1f %1$s should be lesser than or equal to %2$d %1$s should be lesser than or equal to %2$.1f Invalid Mobile Number ! Invalid RCH ID ! Invalid MCP Number ! Invalid Aadhaar Number ! APK Version 2.0.0 Full Name Male Female Transgender Other Other Place of Death Other Reason of Death District

Sub-Center Under You Overdue: 0 Pending Symptomatic TB Screening Check if the person has any of these Symptoms: Asymptomatic Screening Rise of fever in evening Loss of Appetite Age more than 60 years Diabetic Tobacco user BMI < 18.5 Contact with TB patient on treatment Last 5 years history of TB Referral Required Referral For Please screen all family members and close contacts of this beneficiary for TB. TB Screening Referral
Sort By Newest First Oldest First Age Wise Syncing Data First Un-Synced First Cancel Value cannot be empty Enter a valid number Allowed range: %1$d to %2$d Minimum allowed value: %1$d Invalid format. Should be like 123/56 Systole should not be less than %d Systole should not be greater than %d Diastole should not be less than %d Diastole should not be greater than %d Diastole cannot be greater than systole Please enter weight in grams Weight cannot be 0 Please enter weight in grams (e.g. 2500) Weight must be at least 500 grams Weight should not be greater than 7000 grams Are you Sure?
Follow-up date is required or invalid Invalid treatment date in DB Follow-up must be after treatment start date (%s) Follow-up must be in the immediate next month after last follow-up of current visit Follow-up cannot be in a future month Select %s %s is invalid %s must be a number %1$s should be at least %2$s %1$s should be at most %2$s %s date is invalid %1$s cannot be before %2$s %1$s cannot be after %2$s %s is required Main visit not found Discard Changes? Are you sure you want to go back? Changes will be lost. Discard Stay

Unable to save vitals.
